sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After rotating, the character is at [26,40]x[29,43] (horizontal with feet on right), and the green frame is at [29,49]x[41,49] with 108 pixels, plus a separate piece at [32,34]x[41,43] with 9 pixels. Total green is 108 + 9 = 117 pixels.
Wait, but earlier the green frame was 117 pixels as a single object. Now it's 108 + 9 = 117 pixels split into two objects. So the total remained the same, but it split further.
Actually, looking more carefully, the green frame has 108 pixels in the main body and 9 pixels in a separate piece. The total is 117 pixels, which is the same as before. So the green frame didn't break further, it just split into more pieces.
Let me think about this differently. The green frame was 144 pixels when complete. When the character first overlapped, it broke to 126 pixels. When the character rotated while overlapping, it broke to 117 pixels. Now it's 108 + 9 = 117 pixels.
So the green frame broke from 144 to 126 to 117 pixels. The total is 117 now, which is the same as before. So the green frame stopped breaking at 117 pixels.
Wait, but earlier I saw the green frame at 108 pixels. Let me check the history again.
Actually, looking at the segmentation output:
- N: rows [29, 49], cols [41, 49], pixels=108 (main green frame body)
- N: rows [32, 34], cols [41, 43], pixels=9 (separate green piece)
Total: 108 + 9 = 117 pixels.
But earlier, the green frame was 117 pixels as a single object. Now it's split into 108 + 9 = 117 pixels. So the total remained the same.
Hmm, but I also saw the green frame at 108 pixels earlier (when the character was horizontal with feet on left). Let me check the history again.
Actually, I think I'm getting confused. Let me just continue exploring. The character is horizontal with feet on right, overlapping with the green frame. The green frame is at 117 pixels total.
Let me try rotating one more time to complete the cycle and see if the green frame heals or breaks further.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Character horizontal at [26,40]x[29,43] with feet on right, overlapping green frame. Green frame at 108+9=117 pixels total. Green frame broke progressively: 144→126→117.
Plan: Rotate one more time to complete the 4-orientation cycle and see if green frame heals or breaks further.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Rotate to complete cycle
action(['SPACE'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('W', 'N', 'G', 'R'):
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"{n['color']}: rows [{min(rows)}, {max(rows)}], cols [{min(cols)}, {max(cols)}], pixels={n['pixels']}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
